chapter_title.en: What Garments Are Permissible for Prayer and What Garments Are Not

text: 789 - وَ رَوَى اَلْفُضَيْلُ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ - «صَلَّتْ فَاطِمَةُ عَلَيْهَا اَلسَّلاَمُ فِي دِرْعٍ وَ خِمَارُهَا عَلَى رَأْسِهَا لَيْسَ عَلَيْهَا أَكْثَرُ مِمَّا وَارَتْ بِهِ شَعْرَهَا وَ أُذُنَيْهَا.

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.789 - Al-Fudayl narrated from Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) who said:
"Sayyida Fatimah Al-Zahra {S.A} prayed in a tunic with a headscarf covering her head, and She did not have more on than what concealed her hair and her ears”.
